TextENGNew: "...Huff... huff... huff... huff...!!"
"......Shit... oh god, oh god...@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1003. ...Manager...?@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1004. Manager, answer me, please.@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1005. ...Manager......"
"You idiot...@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouB1002. He's obviously dead already...!"
Clang.
...A loud noise rang out, and everyone snapped back to their senses with a jolt.
Someone had dropped the shovel they were holding.
...The blade of the shovel was thickly stained with dark, blackish blood...
And it was not just the shovel.
...The clothes of all six construction workers present were soaked with it as well.
The man calling out to the manager was looking down at a motionless corpse, drenched in blood from head to toe, a gruesome sight...
"...Manager... ...Manager... uuugh...!"
"Shut up...!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1007. Of course he's dead!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1008. ...It couldn't be helped!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1009. It was self-defense, right?! Self-defense...!!"
changeReason: New translation preserves the 'it couldn't be helped' (仕方ねぇじゃねぇか) excuse before the self-defense claim, which the original omits, changing the progression of the ringleader's justification.

TextENGNew: The fight had started because the manager caught them drinking beer in the office.
Signs prohibiting drinking and smoking had been plastered everywhere.
Anyone who broke those rules received a merciless tongue-lashing.
The six workers were undeniably at fault for breaking the rules.
But they had been drinking because the constant hostility of the anti-dam protesters had pushed their stress to the breaking point.
...That was why they had let slip some provocative words toward the manager.
Come outside, they told him.
The others egged them on.
changeReason: New translation more accurately renders 囃し立てた as 'egged them on' (active instigation), whereas original's 'went along' implies passive compliance.

TextENGNew: The grueling construction work offered no amusement whatsoever.
changeReason: New translation accurately captures that the work offered no amusement (面白いことなど何もない), while original reinterprets this as workers toiling hard, missing the point about boredom driving the instigation.

TextENGNew: That was why they found themselves instigating a fight—to let off steam.
At most, they had expected a shouting match, maybe a bit of grappling.
...But things gradually escalated.
Or rather... the manager had erupted with an abnormal intensity of emotion.
...The manager himself had been on the front lines dealing with the residents' hostile protests day after day.
Just as the workers were overwhelmed by stress, it was only natural that the manager had been under tremendous pressure as well.
But even accounting for that... his behavior was beyond all reason.
The manager had been sober when it began—of that, they were certain.
changeReason: New translation correctly renders シラフ as 'sober' (not under the influence of alcohol), while original uses 'calm,' which misses the specific reference to sobriety.

TextENGNew: ...But he grew increasingly unhinged, spiraling into a state of derangement... it was as though he had become a different person entirely... no, as though something "evil" had taken possession of him...
Even the men who had been cheering the fight on sobered up instantly when they witnessed his transformation...
Then...
The manager suddenly snatched up a shovel from beside him and, without a moment's hesitation, swung it at his opponent's head.
The man managed to stumble and fall out of the way just in time, barely avoiding a direct hit to the skull. Had he been a fraction slower, his head would have been split open.
And as the man rolled desperately to escape, the manager brought the shovel down on him again and again without hesitation.
The man scrambled, rolling frantically to dodge the blows.
...By this point, the other men realized this had gone far beyond the realm of a fight, and they moved in to restrain the manager.
But the manager turned on them and raised the shovel high.
One of the men tried to block it by crossing his arms above his head.
...There was no way crossed arms could stop a shovel swung straight down with full force.
Blood spurted out, and a groan of agony escaped the man's lips.
By this point, everyone understood instinctively, beyond any reasoning.
changeReason: New translation accurately renders 理屈抜きで直感していた as 'understood instinctively, beyond any reasoning,' while original merely says 'had realized,' missing the visceral, instinctive nature of the understanding.

TextENGNew: ...The manager was not himself.
The manager showed no restraint whatsoever... if they failed to block or dodge, he would crack their skulls open without mercy...!
To stand against an opponent wielding a shovel... no, simply to defend against a shovel's blows, they needed to arm themselves too.
Seizing an opening, one man grabbed a heavy hammer.
...Seeing that, the others realized they too needed weapons in hand.
It seemed only natural—to subdue the manager in his current state, weapons were indispensable...!
"...This wasn't supposed to happen...@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouC1003. ...Waaaaah..."
For the six men, protecting themselves from the manager's violence was the single highest priority.
changeReason: Old fabricates 'the madness infected the six workers' while JPN says protecting themselves from the manager's violence was the highest priority. Entirely different content.

TextENGNew: ...And as their actions escalated—from defense to restraint, from restraint to counterattack—each step was an inevitable choice born of necessity.
changeReason: New translation preserves the JPN framing of each escalation step as 'an inevitable choice born of necessity' (やむを得ない選択), while original simplistically calls it going from 'restraining to lynching.'

TextENGNew: Before they knew it, the manager... had gone completely still.
changeReason: New translation accurately translates ピクリとも動かなくなっていた as 'gone completely still,' while original's 'already a lump of meat' is a graphic editorialization not in the source text.

TextENGNew: ...He was no longer breathing, and his face bore no resemblance to the man they had known in life.
changeReason: Old fabricates graphic details ('shreds of skin torn off, muscles and skull visible') not present in JPN, which says the face bore no resemblance to the living person.

TextENGNew: ...Everyone realized he was already dead...
"Fuck!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1011. He almost killed us!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1012. If he hadn't attacked us, we wouldn't have fought back!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1013. Right?!"
The ringleader—the one who had started the argument—sought agreement from his companions, but every face was ashen, and no one answered...
Perhaps that displeased him.
...He was the one who had picked the fight.
The others had merely joined in afterward.
...He was the principal offender.
Perhaps that was why he could not bear the thought that the others might view him as a disposable tail to be cut off...
changeReason: New translation preserves the 'lizard's tail' metaphor (トカゲの尻尾), conveying his fear of being treated as expendable, while original misses this entirely with a vague 'exasperated/at fault.'

TextENGNew: "D-Don't just stand there sniveling...!!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1015. It can't be helped!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1016. We killed him, and there's no changing that!!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1017. We're murderers now!!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1018. Steel yourselves!"
changeReason: New translation correctly renders 仕方ねぇ as 'it can't be helped' and 覚悟を決めるんだ as 'steel yourselves,' while original uses less accurate 'it's too late' and 'buck up.'

TextENGNew: "You're not thinking about turning yourselves in and doing time, are you?!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouC1005. Like hell!!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouC1006. I can't go to prison at my age!!"
changeReason: New translation corrects 冗談じゃねぇよ from 'Fuck you' to 'Like hell,' and fixes the number from singular 'yourself' to plural 'yourselves,' better matching the group address.

TextENGNew: "We're hiding it!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1020. We hide this body and act like nothing happened!!"
...Several of them knew that this man was already on probation for a prior assault.
So if the police got involved, the punishment for him would extend far beyond this single incident.
changeReason: New translation accurately conveys 罪はこの件だけでは済まない (punishment wouldn't be limited to just this incident), while original exaggerates to 'more severe than they could imagine.'

TextENGNew: That was why his face was flushed crimson as he screamed, desperately seeking any way out of this situation.
...Had it been just him and the manager, he would not have needed to carry on like this.
...He could have simply hidden the body and feigned ignorance.
But there were six of them, himself included.
There were witnesses who were also accomplices.
If even one of them surrendered to a guilty conscience and turned himself in, the consequences would engulf them all...
"B... But... even if we hide the body... they'll find out eventually..."
"Instead... we should claim self-defense... and maybe they'd let us off with excessive force..."
changeReason: Old says 'they might end up pardoning us' while new says 'maybe they'd let us off with excessive force.' JPN 過剰防衛 means 'excessive self-defense' (a lesser charge). Both versions convey hoping for leniency, but old misrepresents the specific legal concept.

TextENGNew: "Don't be so naive!!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1022. Look at what we did to him!!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1023. Does this look like self-defense?!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1024. His own mother wouldn't recognize him, you idiot!!!"
"B-But... even if we hide it, everyone's going to notice the manager is missing by tomorrow morning.@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouC1008. It's hopeless...!"
"What's hopeless is that brain of yours, you moron!!!"
He slapped the whimpering man across the face.
...At some point, the ringleader's expression had become something demonic...
"Listen up, all of you!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1027. We are murderers! Murderers!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1028. Keep stalling, and we'll all be locked away for five or six years!!
"If even one of us turns traitor!!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1030. Every single one of us goes down!!"

"Don't underestimate prison.@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1032. If you're going to be caught anyway, then run for your lives first!!@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1033. You can resign yourself to fate when the time finally comes!!"
The man's visage grew more and more demonic with every passing moment.
It was the same expression the manager had worn just minutes before.
...Could it be that whatever had seized the manager and transformed him so violently had now taken hold of this man as well...?
The five men truly began to think so.
Come to think of it... that expression was the same one worn by the villagers who raged and howled during the anti-dam demonstrations.
They said the village's true name was Onigafuchi Village.
Legend held that vicious demons had lived there, and that they could possess the living.
...The men could not help but recall that tale, so often invoked by the demonstrators, and for the first time they took it seriously...
changeReason: New translation adds 'for the first time they took it seriously' (from JPN 本気で思い出していた), capturing that the men were now genuinely believing the legend, while original only says they 'recalled' it.

TextENGNew: When this expression reached its apex... the manager had raised the shovel high and unleashed his murderous intent.
......There was no guarantee the man before them would not do the same.
Indeed, he still gripped his hammer, glaring wildly at each of them with menacing eyes.
...If anyone uttered a single word about surrendering to the police...
...he would probably split that man's skull in one blow...

"...That's right.@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1035. ......To make sure nobody betrays the rest.@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1036.I've got an idea.@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1037. ...Heh heh heh.@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1038.It'll make hiding the body easier too—two birds with one stone..."
"Wh...... What kind of plan?"
"Heh heh heh...@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1040. It's obvious, isn't it?@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1041.We're all in this together now. Each of us takes a piece of the evidence and hides it on our own."
changeReason: New translation captures the key phrase 一蓮托生 ('we're all in this together') and correctly describes the plan (each person hides their share), while original has unclear/broken English ('split evidence between each of us and running away').

TextENGNew: "Th-That's..."
They all wanted to object—that was going too far.
The killing alone was a grave crime.
...On top of that, concealing the facts of the crime and fleeing—that was going too far, no matter how you looked at it...
changeReason: Old says 'dismembering the body' while JPN says 'concealing the facts of the crime and fleeing.' Different actions described.

TextENGNew: ...But the atmosphere made it clear that anyone who objected might be beaten to death on the spot...
And so, no further words could escape their throats...

"We are all responsible for this.@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1043.Remember what the manager was always saying?@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1044.Teamwork.@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1045.Everyone, steel yourselves.@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1046.Each of us takes our share of the 'evidence' and hides it responsibly.

"......See?@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1048. This way, every one of us is equally guilty, right?@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1049. Heh heh heh—nobody gets to weasel out by turning themselves in because their share of the blame is lighter.@k@vS20/00/VTK1_sagyouA1050....Heh heh heh heh heh heh!"
changeReason: New translation captures the ズル抜け (weaseling out) nuance and the specific reasoning about lighter blame share, while original simplifies to a general 'I won't let anyone give themselves up.'

TextENGNew: The five men had been completely consumed by his ghastly visage,@k and they could feel the true terror of the demons of Onigafuchi Village creeping up their spines......
